title: Need a new function buildah build --rmi to remove internmediate container images.
body:
A containerfile like the following:
```
FROM alpine  as builder
RUN mkdir -p /models; cd /models; ln -s MODEL model.file

FROM scratch
COPY --from=builder /models /models
```
buildah build -t foobar .

Will build two images, one called foobar and another one as <none><none>

We need to allow users to execute 

buildah build --rmi -t foobar .

Which would remove any cached images created during the build.  Similar to --rm works on containers.

The RamaLama project executes podman build and is leaking these <none><none> images because it is using --nocache during the build.

comments.body: `--layers=false` should not leak any intermediate image afaik
@rhatdan What happens you do `podman build --layers=false` ? Does it still leaks any intermediate image.
Awesome, Thanks @flouthoc That is just what I needed.
Sadly docker build does not have --layers=false, but will use for podman build.
